message:
fix(sync): remove waiter-queue check from try_lock

Remove the !state.waiters.is_empty() guard from try_lock() and
OwnedMutexGuard::try_lock(). The previous behavior rejected
try_lock when waiters existed, ostensibly for fairness, but this
is incorrect for try_lock semantics: try_lock should succeed
whenever the mutex is unlocked, regardless of queued waiters.
The waiter queue is serviced by the async poll path, not by
try_lock callers. Blocking try_lock on waiter presence could
cause livelock when synchronous code needs the lock while
async tasks are queued but not being polled.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
